prompt:
Move the **Export** and **Import Backup** controls from the bottom of the Workouts tab into the Settings tab. They're currently always visible at the bottom of the Workouts UI; they belong with the other configuration/data controls. **Current location:** `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx:341-389` is a bottom toolbar holding five things: - prev-day (`← Back`) - next-day (`Complete & Advance →`) - **export-btn (`Export`) — MOVE this** - **import-backup-label + import-input (`Import Backup` + file input) — MOVE this** - reset-btn (`Reset`) — **LEAVE this for now** (call out in the PR description that it may also belong in Settings; let the reviewer decide as a follow-up) The `importMessage` state + display at `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx:45, 391-398` should move with the import button. **Target location:** `src/tabs/Settings.tsx` currently has Appearance + LLM provider sections. Add a new third section titled **Data** containing Export, Import Backup, and the import message display. Use the same card styling as the other Settings sections (`rounded-lg border border-flux-border bg-flux-card p-4`). **Architecture note — why this is safe across the tab switch:** `src/App.tsx:44-47` conditionally renders tabs (`{active === 'workouts' && }`). When the user switches from Settings back to Workouts after an import, Workouts re-mounts and its effect at `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx:52-60` re-runs `loadState() + loadLog() + loadProgram()`, picking up the fresh data from IDB. So Settings just needs to write to IDB via the existing `./logic/exportImport` helpers — no cross-tab event system or state lift needed. **Implementation steps:** 1. **In `src/tabs/Settings.tsx`:** - Import `exportPayload`, `downloadExport`, `applyImportFromFile`, `formatImportMessage` from `'../tabs/Workouts/logic/exportImport'` (adjust path; the module is at `src/tabs/Workouts/logic/exportImport.ts`). If those names are wrong, read the file to find the right ones. - Add `const [importMessage, setImportMessage] = useState(null);` - Add async `handleExport` and `handleImport(e: Event)` — copy verbatim from `Workouts/index.tsx:162-184`, BUT remove the `loadState/loadLog/loadProgram + setState/setLog/setProgram` block from handleImport (Settings doesn't hold workout state — the re-mount-on-switch handles refresh). Replace with just `setImportMessage(formatImportMessage(result))` on success and the same error path on failure. - Add a new section after the LLM provider card: ```tsx

Data

{importMessage && (

{importMessage}

)}
``` - **Preserve the exact `data-testid` values**: `export-btn`, `import-backup-label`, `import-input`, `import-message`. Tests depend on these. 2. **In `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx`:** - Remove the `importMessage` state (line 45) and its setter calls - Remove the export button, import-backup-label, and import-input from the bottom toolbar (lines 360-380) - Remove the import-message display (lines 391-398) - Remove the now-unused `handleExport` and `handleImport` functions (lines 162-184) - Remove unused imports from `./logic/exportImport` (keep any that are still used by the program import flow at line 186+) - Keep prev-day, next-day, and reset-btn in the toolbar. The bottom toolbar will now have just three buttons (and a flex spacer) instead of five. 3. **Update existing e2e tests** that click these controls — they currently assume they're on the Workouts tab. Update each test to navigate to Settings first via `page.locator('[data-tab="settings"]').click()` before clicking export/import. Files to update: - `tests/import-export.spec.ts` — uses `import-input`, `import-message`, `export-btn` - `tests/import.spec.ts` — uses `import-input`, `import-message`, `import-backup-label` - Look at `tests/workouts.spec.ts` for existing patterns of tab navigation. The tab nav uses `[data-tab=""]` per `src/App.tsx:63`. After import in tests, if a test needs to verify the imported data shows up in Workouts, navigate back to Workouts after import: `await page.locator('[data-tab="workouts"]').click()` — the Workouts tab will re-mount and reload from IDB. **Validation (all three must pass):** - `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test --project=chromium` — full e2e suite passes including the updated import/export tests - `nix develop --command npm run typecheck` — no TS errors - `nix develop --command bash -c "npm ci && npm run build"` — production build succeeds **Manual sanity check (you can describe in PR body without actually running):** the Workouts bottom toolbar should now show only prev-day / next-day / reset; Settings should show three cards (Appearance, LLM provider, Data) with Export and Import Backup under Data. **Out of scope:** - Do NOT move the Reset button (call it out in the PR description as a possible follow-up — user may want it moved too) - Do NOT touch the program-import flow (the `import-program-btn` and `import-program-input` at `src/tabs/Workouts/index.tsx:256, 275` are separate from backup import — leave them in Workouts; they're program-specific) - Do NOT restyle the existing Settings sections - Do NOT add new features (search, filtering, etc.) **PR title:** "settings: move Export / Import Backup from Workouts toolbar into Settings" **PR body:** brief description of the move, the architecture note about tab re-mount handling refresh, and an explicit follow-up question: "Reset is still in the Workouts toolbar — should it also move to Settings under Data? It's destructive and groups naturally with backup. Left for separate decision."
